testcase.name: dev > server process > spawns tsx from the project node_modules/.bin with --watch src/server.ts
dev > server process > prepends --inspect=0.0.0.0:9229 to tsx args when --inspect is passed
dev > server process > passes db env vars into the tsx process environment
dev > server process > includes project node_modules/.bin in PATH for the server process
dev > database lifecycle > calls detectDatabases before starting the server
dev > database lifecycle > passes the detected config to startDatabases
dev > database lifecycle > calls server.stop() on each started database after the server exits
dev > database lifecycle > does not throw when no databases are configured
dev > React / Vite integration > does not spawn vite when detectReact returns false
dev > React / Vite integration > spawns vite build --watch concurrently when detectReact returns true
dev > React / Vite integration > vite process receives the same db env vars as the server
start > --no-build flag > skips the build step entirely and goes straight to starting the server
start > --no-build flag > does not call detectReact when --no-build is set
start > build step (without --no-build) > runs npm run build when no yarn.lock or packageManager field exists
start > build step (without --no-build) > runs yarn build when yarn.lock is present
start > build step (without --no-build) > runs yarn build when packageManager field starts with "yarn"
start > server process > spawns node with a server.js path
start > server process > checks for server files relative to the project cwd, not the node binary path
start > server process > falls back to dist/server.js when no specific layout is detected
start > server process > uses dist/server/server.js when existsSync returns true for that path (first check)
start > server process > uses dist/src/server.js when the first check fails but the second succeeds
start > server process > passes db env vars into the server process environment
start > database lifecycle > calls detectDatabases before starting the server
start > database lifecycle > passes the detected config to startDatabases
start > database lifecycle > calls server.stop() on each started database after server exits
start > React / Vite integration > does not run vite build when detectReact returns false
start > React / Vite integration > runs vite build between the main build and server startup when detectReact is true
start > React / Vite integration > skips vite build when --no-build is set even if detectReact would return true
detectDatabases > detects mongodb from type: "mongodb" in datastores
detectDatabases > detects redis from type: "redis" in datastores
detectDatabases > detects postgresql from type: "postgresql" in datastores
detectDatabases > detects multiple database types from a single config
detectDatabases > handles single-quoted type values
detectDatabases > returns all false when config has no datastore type strings
detectDatabases > returns all false when src/config.ts does not exist
startDatabases > starts mongodb-memory-server and sets DATASTORES env vars
startDatabases > sets no env vars and spawns no processes when no databases configured
startDatabases > starts redis-memory-server and sets DATASTORES env vars
startDatabases > starts postgres-memory-server and sets DATASTORES env vars
tsBlockInsert > inserts a snippet before the closing brace of the named block
tsBlockInsert > is a no-op when idempotencyKey already appears as a depth-1 property
tsBlockInsert > inserts when idempotencyKey does not match any existing property
tsBlockInsert > throws a descriptive error when the named block does not exist
tsBlockInsert > handles an empty block
tsBlockInsert > does not match property names inside string literals
tsBlockInsert > skips property names inside // comments when checking idempotency
tsPropertySet > replaces the value of the named property inside the block
tsPropertySet > is a no-op when the value is already the desired value
tsPropertySet > preserves all other content outside the changed property
tsPropertySet > throws when the named block does not exist
tsPropertySet > returns source unchanged when the property does not exist in the block
tsPropertySet > does not affect same-named properties inside sibling blocks
tsPropertySet > handles an object-valued property without corrupting it
tsPropertySet > skips // comments when scanning for property names
jsonMerge > merges dependencies into an existing package.json object
jsonMerge > patch value wins on scalar conflicts
jsonMerge > deep-merges nested objects independently
jsonMerge > replaces arrays rather than concatenating
jsonMerge > is idempotent: applying the same patch twice yields the same result
jsonMerge > does not mutate the target argument
jsonMerge > handles an empty patch (no-op)
applyPatches > ts-block-insert: inserts rendered snippet into the named block
applyPatches > ts-block-insert: skips insertion when idempotencyKey is already present
applyPatches > ts-block-insert: throws when target file does not exist
applyPatches > json-merge: merges dependency entries into existing package.json
applyPatches > json-merge: creates the file if it does not exist
applyPatches > json-merge: is idempotent — running twice yields the same result
applyPatches > ts-property-set: replaces a property value in the named block
applyPatches > ts-property-set: is idempotent when property already has the desired value
applyPatches > skips a patch entirely when condition evaluates to falsy
detectReact > returns true when vite.config.ts exists
detectReact > returns false when vite.config.ts does not exist
detectReact > returns false for a non-existent directory
readProjectAuthor > returns a string author field
readProjectAuthor > returns the name from an object author field
readProjectAuthor > returns undefined when author is an empty string
readProjectAuthor > returns undefined when author field is absent
readProjectAuthor > returns undefined when package.json does not exist
readProjectAuthor > returns undefined when package.json is invalid JSON
extractDatastoreInfo > extracts name and type for each top-level datastore property
extractDatastoreInfo > handles multi-line datastore objects
extractDatastoreInfo > returns an empty array when there is no datastores block
extractDatastoreInfo > returns empty type when the datastore object has no type field
extractDatastoreInfo > does not confuse nested type fields with the top-level one
extractDatastoreInfo > ignores type values inside string literals
extractDatastoreInfo > skips // line comments
readProjectDatastores > reads datastores from src/config.ts
readProjectDatastores > returns an empty array when src/config.ts does not exist
extractModelDatastore > extracts the datastore name from a @DataStore decorator
extractModelDatastore > handles double-quoted strings
extractModelDatastore > returns an empty string when no @DataStore decorator is present
readProjectModels > returns the class name (filename without .ts) for each model file
readProjectModels > excludes .d.ts declaration files
readProjectModels > returns an empty array when src/models does not exist
readModelDatastore > reads the @DataStore value from the named model file
readModelDatastore > returns an empty string when the model file does not exist
readModelDatastore > returns an empty string when the model has no @DataStore decorator
inputAuthor > always calls input() and returns whatever the user enters
inputAuthor > uses git config author as the default value for the prompt
inputAuthor > uses package.json author as the default when git config is not set and cwd is provided
inputAuthor > prefers git config over package.json when both are available
inputAuthor > uses undefined as the default when neither git config nor package.json has an author
inputAuthor > does not call readProjectAuthor when no cwd is provided
inputAuthor > does not call readProjectAuthor when git config returns an author
inputAuthor > passes the correct message and required flag to input()
generate route — route template > generates the route file and no scaffolding artifacts
generate route — route template > generates a model-extended route when model is provided
generate route — route template > generates a plain route (no model class) when model is empty
generate route — test template > generates the test file
generate route — test template > includes mongo-specific imports when datastoreType is mongodb
generate route — test template > omits mongo-specific imports when datastoreType is not mongodb
generate model > generates the model file under src/models/ and no scaffolding artifacts
generate model > emits Mongo entity imports when datastoreType is mongodb
generate model > emits SQL entity imports when datastoreType is not mongodb
generate react > generates app files, route file, and vite config with no scaffolding artifacts
generate react > merges react dependencies into package.json via patch
generate server > generates core server files when all optional features are disabled
generate server > includes GitHub CI workflow when scm.github is enabled
generate server > includes database model and routes when features.hasDatabase is enabled
generate server > does not include react app files when features.react is disabled
processTemplate > substitutes {{variable}} in file content
processTemplate > substitutes {{name}} placeholders in output file paths
processTemplate > evaluates {{#if}} Handlebars conditionals in content
processTemplate > copies binary files (.cjs, .gz) byte-for-byte without Handlebars processing
processTemplate > excludes a file when its manifest condition is false
processTemplate > includes a file when its manifest condition is true
processTemplate > excludes a directory subtree when its manifest condition is false
processTemplate > preserves ${{ }} GitHub Actions syntax unchanged
processTemplate > throws when an output file already exists and force is not set
processTemplate > overwrites an existing output file when force is true
processTemplate > creates nested output directories for files in subdirectories
processTemplate > patches > does not copy patch template files into the output directory
processTemplate > patches > applies a json-merge patch to the project directory after file generation
processTemplate > patches > skips a patch when its condition is falsy in the context
processTemplate > patches > applies a ts-block-insert patch to src/config.ts in the project directory
processTemplate > Helm-safe mode (helmPaths) > substitutes [[ varname ]] in Helm files
processTemplate > Helm-safe mode (helmPaths) > resolves [[#if condition]]...[[/if]] blocks in Helm files
processTemplate > Helm-safe mode (helmPaths) > preserves Helm {{ }} Go-template expressions in helmPaths files
processTemplate > Helm-safe mode (helmPaths) > handles nested [[#if]] blocks in Helm files
processTemplate > Helm-safe mode (helmPaths) > substitutes [[ dotted.path ]] from nested context in Helm files
processTemplate > Helm-safe mode (helmPaths) > files outside helmPaths still use Handlebars {{ }} normally
generate docker > context building > passes datastores from the project config into context
generate docker > context building > includes the current year in context
generate docker > context building > includes project_name from package.json in context
generate docker > database boolean flags > sets hasMongoDB true when any datastore type is mongodb
generate docker > database boolean flags > sets hasPostgres true when any datastore type is postgresql
generate docker > database boolean flags > sets hasRedis true when any datastore type is redis
generate docker > database boolean flags > sets all boolean flags true when all three types are present
generate docker > database boolean flags > sets all boolean flags false when datastores is empty
generate docker > database boolean flags > ignores unknown datastore types for boolean flags
generate docker > output and template options > uses process.cwd() as the output directory when --output-dir is not set
generate docker > output and template options > uses --output-dir as the output directory and project source when provided
generate docker > output and template options > points processTemplate at the docker template directory
generate docker > output and template options > passes force: true to processTemplate when --force is set
generate docker > output and template options > passes projectDir: process.cwd() to processTemplate
generate docker > output and template options > calls readProjectDatastores with the current working directory
generate docker > output and template options > calls readProjectName with the current working directory
generate model > context building > builds the correct context from prompts and passes it to processTemplate
generate model > context building > includes cache: false and protect: false when those prompts answer no
generate model > context building > sets isMongoDb true and other db booleans false when datastoreType is mongodb
generate model > context building > includes project_name from package.json in the context
generate model > datastore selection — configured datastores present > shows only non-acl datastores in the select choices, plus a new option
generate model > datastore selection — configured datastores present > resolves datastoreType from the config list for the selected name
generate model > datastore selection — configured datastores present > selecting "+ New datastore..." prompts for db type and uses it as both name and type
generate model > datastore selection — configured datastores present > does not show the "set up new database" prompt when datastores are configured
generate model > datastore selection — no configured datastores > asks to set up a new database and selects the type when the user says yes
generate model > datastore selection — no configured datastores > sets datastore and datastoreType to empty string when the user declines
generate model > flag shortcuts bypass prompts > --datastore skips the datastore select but still resolves datastoreType from config
generate model > flag shortcuts bypass prompts > --datastore leaves datastoreType empty when the name is not in the project config
generate model > flag shortcuts bypass prompts > --description skips the description input prompt
generate model > flag shortcuts bypass prompts > --cache skips the cache select prompt
generate model > flag shortcuts bypass prompts > --protect skips the protect select prompt
generate model > flag shortcuts bypass prompts > --author skips inputAuthor entirely
generate model > author resolution > calls inputAuthor with the project cwd and uses its return value
generate model > output and template options > uses ./src/models as the default output directory
generate model > output and template options > passes force: true to processTemplate when --force is set
generate model > output and template options > points processTemplate at the model template directory
generate react > context building > builds the correct context from prompts and passes it to processTemplate
generate react > context building > includes hydrate: false when the hydrate select answers no
generate react > context building > includes project_name from package.json in the context
generate react > context building > uses the app name as the default suggestion for the path prompt
generate react > flag shortcuts bypass prompts > --path skips the path input prompt
generate react > flag shortcuts bypass prompts > --hydrate skips the hydrate select prompt
generate react > flag shortcuts bypass prompts > --author skips inputAuthor entirely
generate react > author resolution > calls inputAuthor with the project cwd and uses its return value
generate react > output and template options > uses the project root as the default output directory
generate react > output and template options > passes force: true to processTemplate when --force is set
generate react > output and template options > passes projectDir: process.cwd() to processTemplate
generate react > output and template options > points processTemplate at the react template directory
generate route > context building > builds the correct context from prompts and passes it to processTemplate
generate route > context building > resolves datastoreType from the model's @DataStore decorator via the project config
generate route > context building > sets datastore and datastoreType to empty when model is "(none)"
generate route > context building > leaves datastoreType empty when the model has no @DataStore decorator
generate route > model selection > shows a select with "(none)", project models, and a "+ New model..." option
generate route > model selection > runs generate model inline when "+ New model..." is selected and uses the newly created model
generate route > model selection > falls back to a free-form input when no models exist in the project
generate route > model selection > --no-model skips all model prompts and leaves model undefined in context
generate route > flag shortcuts bypass prompts > --model skips the model select and resolves datastore from the named model file
generate route > flag shortcuts bypass prompts > --description skips the description input prompt
generate route > flag shortcuts bypass prompts > --path skips the route path input prompt
generate route > flag shortcuts bypass prompts > --protect skips the protect select prompt
generate route > flag shortcuts bypass prompts > --author skips inputAuthor entirely
generate route > author resolution > calls inputAuthor with the project cwd and uses its return value
generate route > output and template options > uses project root as the default output directory for the route file
generate route > output and template options > passes force: true to both processTemplate calls when --force is set
generate server > builds the correct base context from prompts
generate server > maps selected database and feature choices to boolean flags on context.features
generate server > sets hasDatabase true when any persistent store is selected
generate server > sets hasDatabase false when only non-persistent features are selected
generate server > maps the SCM choice to a boolean map on context.scm
generate server > maps the package manager choice to a boolean map on context.pkgMgr
generate server > uses ./<name> as the default output directory
generate server > uses --output-dir when provided
generate server > points processTemplate at the server template directory
generate server > passes force: true when --force is set
generate server > author resolution > calls inputAuthor and uses its return value as the author
generate server > docker subcommand > runs GenerateDocker with --output-dir after server generation when docker is selected
generate server > docker subcommand > does not run GenerateDocker when docker is not selected
generate server > docker subcommand > passes --force to GenerateDocker when --force is set on the server command
generate server > docker subcommand > uses the default output directory (./<name>) when --output-dir is not set
